title: On-Policy Distillation
description: A DeepSeek-V4 post-training routine where the student is refined on trajectories produced under its current policy rather than only on frozen offline traces.
openingSummary: Offline distillation can drift away from the states a student model actually visits. On-policy distillation closes that gap by training on trajectories produced under the current model policy, so the supervision stays closer to the behavior you are trying to shape.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: This is a distillation routine used in the DeepSeek-V4 report. The important idea is that data is refreshed from the model's own current behavior rather than treated as one fixed offline replay set.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: It aims to improve policy consistency and reduce the mismatch between training supervision and the states the student actually enters at inference time.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: A current policy samples trajectories, those trajectories are scored or filtered, and the student is updated on the accepted behavior. The loop then repeats with the new policy.

comparedToNearbyRegimes.title: Compared To Nearby Regimes
comparedToNearbyRegimes.body: Unlike plain offline distillation, the training set is not fully frozen. Unlike full RL, the emphasis is still on teacher-guided or filtered behavior rather than only scalar reward optimization.

limitationsAndFailureModes.title: Limitations And Failure Modes
limitationsAndFailureModes.body: If the current policy explores badly, the loop can reinforce narrow behavior. The regime also depends on good filtering or teacher signals.

trainingFlow.title: On-Policy Distillation training flow
trainingFlow.alt: A loop from current policy to sampled trajectories to student update.
trainingFlow.caption: On-policy distillation keeps refreshing supervision from the student's current behavior.
